L1.area: Archive extraction and data dump processing pipeline

L2.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists in the archive extraction logic. The archive path sanitization check uses a string prefix comparison that does not include a trailing path separator, allowing a crafted tar archive entry to bypass the directory containment check and write files outside the intended destination directory.

L3.area: Tar archive extraction and archive path sanitization within the data extraction subsystem
L3.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists in the archive path sanitization logic used during tar extraction. The string prefix check on the resolved file path does not append a trailing path separator to the destination directory, causing a directory name prefix collision that allows crafted tar entries to write files outside the intended extraction directory.

ground_truth.reason: The sanitizeArchivePath function uses strings.HasPrefix without appending a trailing path separator to the destination directory, enabling a crafted tar archive to write files outside the intended extraction directory via directory name prefix collision.
